statement.markdown: Write a function to validate a Git tag name. A valid tag name must start with `v` followed by a sequence of digits and dots. For example, `v1.2.3` is a valid tag name, while `abc` or `v123` are not. Your function should return `true` if the tag name is valid, and `false` otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the tag name to be validated
statement.outputFormat: A boolean value indicating whether the tag name is valid
statement.notes: The tag name will always be non-empty.

examples.explanation: A valid tag name with multiple digits and dots
An invalid tag name with non-digit characters
An invalid tag name without dots

hints.text: Use a regular expression to match the tag name pattern

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing Git Tag Name Validation
editorial.intuition: The correct approach is to use a regular expression to match the tag name pattern
editorial.bruteForce: You can use a brute force approach to check each character of the tag name
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution is to use a regular expression to match the tag name pattern
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a regular expression to match the tag name pattern
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The regular expression `^v\d+(?:\.\d+)*$` matches any string that starts with `v`, followed by one or more digits, optionally followed by one or more dots and digits
commonMistakes: Not using a regular expression to match the tag name pattern
